question: Which of the following BEST describes the observed tricuspid valve pathology?
option_A: The tricuspid valve leaflets are thickened and stenotic, causing restricted blood flow from the right atrium to the right ventricle.
option_B: The tricuspid valve leaflets are prolapsing into the right atrium during systole, leading to moderate regurgitation.
option_C: The tricuspid valve leaflets exhibit failure to fully coapt, resulting in severe tricuspid regurgitation.
option_D: The tricuspid valve appears structurally normal.
